text: I feel a lingering sense of confusion and vulnerability from the words I just heard—"You caught the wrong person"—echoing as reassurance tries to patch over unease. This modern, glass-filled space reflects my uncertain presence, a participant mistaken in a system too mechanical to recognize my individuality. In the next room, facing the wall of 'R&D' and innovation, I feel small—a curious observer among bright screens and conceptual futures. I sense a tension between my raw, momentary vulnerability and the sterility of human progress displayed before me. Am I okay? In these places of confusion and invention, I teeter between discomfort and a curiosity to move forward.
